notes: BATTERIES category (cat_batteries) and category_regulations for GPSR (mandatory=1) and EPR (mandatory=1) already exist.
EPR_REPAIR_INSTRUCTIONS already covers repair_instructions_json for BATTERIES (category_codes: [ELECTRONICS, BATTERIES], warning).
The EU Battery Regulation (EU) 2023/1542 is the primary instrument — it replaces Directive 2006/66/EC (Battery Directive). Full replacement as of August 18 2025.

Battery categories under 2023/1542: portable (<5kg), LMT (Light Means of Transport), SLI (Starting, Lighting, Ignition), industrial (≥5kg), electric vehicle (EV). Rules here are scoped to the BATTERIES product category broadly; product-sub-type tagging does not yet exist in the schema.
Phasing of key Battery Regulation requirements (approximate dates; refer to official text for precise application dates):
- Labelling (Art. 13): Aug 2024 (general) — capacity, chemistry, hazard symbols, QR code
- Carbon footprint declaration (Art. 38): EV batteries Aug 2025; LMT batteries Aug 2025; industrial ≥2kWh Aug 2025
- Battery passport (Art. 14): EV batteries Feb 2027; industrial ≥2kWh Aug 2030; LMT batteries Aug 2026
- Recycled content minimum (Art. 8): Co 16%, Pb 85%, Li 6%, Ni 6% — from 2031 (first threshold)
Carbon footprint and battery passport obligations are phased and battery-category-specific. Rules for these are marked requires_human_review and severity='info'.

WEEE Directive (2012/19/EU) also applies to waste batteries placed on the market; 2023/1542 EPR provisions (Art. 67–70) supersede the battery-specific parts of the Battery Directive but WEEE still applies for collection infrastructure.
UN 38.3 transport testing (lithium batteries) is a transport safety requirement, not a product information/DPP requirement — excluded.

new_regulations_required.name: EU Battery Regulation
new_regulations_required.description: Regulation (EU) 2023/1542. Comprehensive lifecycle requirements for batteries: labelling, safety, EPR, carbon footprint, battery passport. Replaces Directive 2006/66/EC from 18 August 2025.

new_regulations_required.name: Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals
new_regulations_required.description: Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006. SVHC obligations apply to battery articles; cobalt and lithium are subject to authorisation/restriction scrutiny.

new_regulations_required.name: Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment Directive
new_regulations_required.description: Directive 2012/19/EU. Applies to batteries embedded in or supplied with EEE. Collection and separate disposal obligations.

category_regulations_required.note: Battery Regulation (EU) 2023/1542 is mandatory for all batteries placed on the EU market. Replaces the Battery Directive from August 2025.
REACH SVHC obligations apply to battery articles. Cobalt, lithium compounds, and nickel sulfate are on or near the SVHC Candidate List.
WEEE applies to batteries in or supplied with EEE. Collection infrastructure and labelling obligations.

rules.message_en: Battery chemistry must be declared (e.g. Li-ion, NiMH, LFP, lead-acid, alkaline). Required on the label and in accompanying information per Battery Regulation Art. 13(2)(b). This information must be accessible via QR code from August 2025.
rules.message_fi: Akkukemia on ilmoitettava (esim. Li-ion, NiMH, LFP, lyijyhappo, alkali). Vaaditaan etiketissä ja mukana toimitetuissa tiedoissa akkuasetuksen Art. 13(2)(b) mukaisesti. Nämä tiedot on oltava QR-koodin kautta saatavissa elokuusta 2025 alkaen.
rules.legal_reference: Regulation (EU) 2023/1542, Art. 13(2)(b) — labelling of battery chemistry; Art. 13(6) — QR code access to data; Annex VI — labelling requirements

rules.message_en: Hazardous substance content must be declared for batteries. Minimum: declare presence/absence and concentration of: cadmium (>0.002%), lead (>0.004%), mercury (>0.0005%). Also declare cobalt, lithium, and nickel content to support supply chain due diligence (Battery Regulation Art. 10, 13(5)(e)).
rules.message_fi: Akkujen vaarallisten aineiden pitoisuus on ilmoitettava. Vähintään: ilmoita kadmiumin (>0,002 %), lyijyn (>0,004 %) ja elohopean (>0,0005 %) esiintyminen/puuttuminen ja pitoisuus. Ilmoita myös koboltti-, litium- ja nikkelipitoisuus toimitusketjun huolellisuuden tueksi (akkuasetus Art. 10, 13(5)(e)).
rules.legal_reference: Regulation (EU) 2023/1542, Art. 6 — restrictions on hazardous substances (Hg, Cd thresholds); Art. 10 — substances of concern (Co, Pb, Li, Ni); Art. 13(5)(e) — labelling of hazardous substance content; Annex XIII — substances of concern

rules.message_en: Safety warnings are mandatory on battery labels and in accompanying information: disposal and recycling symbol (crossed-out wheeled bin), chemical hazard symbols where applicable (GHS pictograms), and instructions that the battery should be kept away from heat sources. State capacity and rated voltage.
rules.message_fi: Turvallisuusvaroitukset ovat pakollisia akkuetiketeissä ja mukana toimitetuissa tiedoissa: hävittämis- ja kierrätyssymboli (yliviivattu roskakori), kemiallisten vaarojen symbolit tapauksen mukaan (GHS-kuvakkeet) sekä ohjeet siitä, ettei akkua saa altistaa lämmölle. Ilmoita kapasiteetti ja nimellisjännite.
rules.legal_reference: Regulation (EU) 2023/1542, Art. 11 — general safety requirements; Art. 13(2) — labelling content; Annex VI — label content including crossed-out bin symbol; (EC) No 1272/2008 (CLP) — GHS hazard symbols for chemical content

rules.message_en: End-of-life collection and recycling instructions are mandatory under the Battery Regulation EPR scheme (Art. 67-70). Inform users: (a) the battery must be separately collected, (b) free take-back is available, (c) where to return the battery (collection points, retailers, producers). Include the crossed-out wheeled bin symbol reference.
rules.message_fi: Elinkaaren lopun keräys- ja kierrätysohjeet ovat pakollisia akkuasetuksen laajennetun tuottajavastuun järjestelmässä (Art. 67–70). Tiedota käyttäjiä: (a) akku on kerättävä erikseen, (b) ilmainen palautus on saatavilla, (c) mihin akku palautetaan (keräyspisteet, vähittäiskauppiaat, tuottajat). Sisällytä viittaus yliviivattu roskakori -symboliin.
rules.legal_reference: Regulation (EU) 2023/1542, Art. 67-70 (EPR obligations); Art. 74(2) — duty to inform end-users about separate collection; Art. 13(2)(a) — crossed-out wheeled bin labelling

rules.message_en: An EU Declaration of Conformity is required for batteries placed on the EU market under the Battery Regulation (Art. 18) and any co-applicable directive (e.g. LVD for battery packs with integrated electronics). The DoC must reference Regulation (EU) 2023/1542 and confirm conformity with all applicable requirements.
rules.message_fi: EU-vaatimustenmukaisuusvakuutus vaaditaan EU:n markkinoille saatettaville akuille akkuasetuksen (Art. 18) ja kaikkien muiden sovellettavien direktiivien (esim. LVD integroitua elektroniikkaa sisältäville akku-paketeille) nojalla. Vakuutuksen on viitattava asetukseen (EU) 2023/1542 ja vahvistettava vaatimustenmukaisuus kaikkien sovellettavien vaatimusten osalta.
rules.legal_reference: Regulation (EU) 2023/1542, Art. 18 — EU declaration of conformity; Art. 19 — CE marking (where applicable); Annex VIII — EU DoC template

rules.message_en: Batteries may contain SVHC candidates: cobalt compounds (cobalt(II) sulphate is on the Candidate List), nickel sulphate, and lithium compounds are under REACH scrutiny. Declare any SVHC present above 0.1% w/w to enable downstream communication obligations under REACH Art. 33.
rules.message_fi: Akut saattavat sisältää SVHC-ehdokkaita: koboltinyhdisteet (koboltti(II)sulfaatti on ehdokaslistalla), nikkeli(II)sulfaatti ja litiumyhdisteet ovat REACH-tarkastelun kohteena. Ilmoita kaikki SVHC-aineet, joita esiintyy yli 0,1 % (p/p) pitoisuudella, REACH Art. 33 -mukaisten toimitusketjun viestintävelvoitteiden mahdollistamiseksi.
rules.legal_reference: REACH Art. 33 (SVHC communication in articles); ECHA SVHC Candidate List — cobalt(II) sulphate (EC 233-334-2), nickel sulphate (EC 232-104-9); (EC) No 1907/2006

rules.message_en: (Phased requirement — verify applicability) Carbon footprint declarations are required under Battery Regulation Art. 38 for EV batteries (from August 2025), LMT batteries (from August 2025), and industrial batteries ≥2kWh (from August 2025). Portable batteries are NOT currently subject to this requirement. Upload the carbon footprint declaration if your battery type is in scope.
rules.message_fi: (Vaiheistettu vaatimus — tarkista sovellettavuus) Hiilijalanjälki-ilmoitukset ovat pakollisia akkuasetuksen Art. 38:n nojalla sähköajoneuvojen akuille (elokuusta 2025), LMT-akuille (elokuusta 2025) ja teollisuusakuille ≥2kWh (elokuusta 2025). Kannettaville akuille ei tällä hetkellä ole tätä vaatimusta. Lataa hiilijalanjälki-ilmoitus, jos akkutyyppisi kuuluu soveltamisalaan.
rules.legal_reference: Regulation (EU) 2023/1542, Art. 38 — carbon footprint declarations; Art. 39 — carbon footprint performance classes; Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2024/1682 (carbon footprint calculation method)
rules.human_review_reason: Carbon footprint obligation depends on battery sub-type (EV, LMT, industrial, portable) and precise application date of Commission implementing regulations. The current schema has no battery sub-type field, making reliable automated evaluation impossible. Severity set to 'info' only. A future rule should be gated on a battery_subtype field.
